channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: A Philippine senator wanted by the International Criminal Court flees from Senate
item.description: A police investigation is underway in the Philippines, with suspicions that the incident was staged to help Sen. Ronald dela Rosa escape.
item.content:encoded:
A police investigation is underway in the Philippines, with suspicions that the incident was staged to help Sen. Ronald dela Rosa escape.

(Image credit: Aaron Favila)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: An LA-area mayor acted as an agent for China. Experts say it's part of a pattern
item.description: Eileen Wang, now the former mayor of the City of Arcadia, agreed to plead guilty to one felony charge that she acted as an illegal foreign agent of China.
item.content:encoded:
Eileen Wang, now the former mayor of the City of Arcadia, agreed to plead guilty to one felony charge that she acted as an illegal foreign agent of China.

(Image credit: Frazer Harrison)
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Romo

item.title: Trump tries to make deals in China. And, Senate confirms Kevin Warsh as Fed leader
item.description: President Trump is vying to make deals with China during his visit with President Xi Jinping. And, the Senate confirmed Kevin Warsh as the next leader of the Federal Reserve.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump is vying to make deals with China during his visit with President Xi Jinping. And, the Senate confirmed Kevin Warsh as the next leader of the Federal Reserve.

(Image credit: Alex Wong)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: China's leader warns Trump that differences over Taiwan could lead to a clash
item.description: At the summit, the Chinese leader placed Taiwan, a key point of friction between the U.S. and China, at the center, calling it "the most important issue" between the two countries.
item.content:encoded:
At the summit, the Chinese leader placed Taiwan, a key point of friction between the U.S. and China, at the center, calling it "the most important issue" between the two countries.

(Image credit: China pool)
item.dc:creator: Jan Camenzind Broomby

item.title: A brain-controlled system may help listeners with hearing loss cut through the noise
item.description: A hearing system that monitors brain waves could help people with hearing loss communicate in noisy environments.
item.content:encoded:
A hearing system that monitors brain waves could help people with hearing loss communicate in noisy environments.

(Image credit: Matteo Farinella)
item.dc:creator: Jon Hamilton

item.title: Ex-DOJ official goes public with blistering criticism of his former bosses
item.description: Until recently, Jonathan Gross was a Trump political appointee at the Department of Justice and worked on its "Weaponization Working Group." He has now become a vocal critic of the department.
item.content:encoded: Until recently, Jonathan Gross was a Trump political appointee at the Department of Justice and worked on its "Weaponization Working Group." He has now become a vocal critic of the department.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dreisbach

item.title: Voters are caught in the middle as the redistricting battle intensifies
item.description: Much of the focus of the ongoing redistricting war has been on which political party will come out on top. But it's voters who will pay a cost, say voting experts and voting rights advocates.
item.content:encoded:
Much of the focus of the ongoing redistricting war has been on which political party will come out on top. But it's voters who will pay a cost, say voting experts and voting rights advocates.

(Image credit: Win McNamee)
item.dc:creator: Ashley Lopez

item.title: The MAHA movement is coming to school cafeterias. Here's what that means for kids
item.description: U.S. school districts worry it could get even more expensive to prepare a meal under new federal dietary guidelines, as they also contend with cuts to programs that helped them buy local food.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. school districts worry it could get even more expensive to prepare a meal under new federal dietary guidelines, as they also contend with cuts to programs that helped them buy local food.

(Image credit: Rachel Wisniewski for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Joe Hernandez

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: President Trump met with Chinese President Xi Jingping for summit during first day in the country, what Asia thinks of Trump's visit, appeals court hears arguments from law firms targeted by Trump.
item.content:encoded: President Trump met with Chinese President Xi Jingping for summit during first day in the country, what Asia thinks of Trump's visit, appeals court hears arguments from law firms targeted by Trump.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: How Asia is reacting to Trump's summit in China
item.description: President Trump is in Beijing for his big summit with Chinese leaders. How Asia is reacting to the summit.
item.content:encoded: President Trump is in Beijing for his big summit with Chinese leaders. How Asia is reacting to the summit.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep
